def: s. f. See اوشده.
n. f. from au bad, kah speak. Rude or harsh language; a crooked answer.
s.m. Any great calamity, (as a) plague, cholera, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوَّل (awwal), borrowed from Arabic أَوَّل (ʔawwal).

def: adj. = اولٰي aulā, q.v.
more: adj. Better, best: see اوليٰٰ aulạ̄. ¶ awwalaṉ, adv. At first, in the first place.

def: adj. (compar. & superl. of walī), Better; best; superior, excellent
more: adj. Best; superior; better; excellent. Marzī-i-Maulā sab se aulā . Prov. What God wills is best.

def: adj. & s.m. = ava-lakshaṇ, q.v. adj. Of inauspicious or evil omen; — unfortunate; — s.m. Bad omen.
s.f. Legitimate children
more: s. f. (plur. of ولد) Children, offspring, descendants, issue, progeny.
subs: s.f. Children in a directline, lineal descendants
s.f. Legitimate children or issue ( = aulāduʼl-ḥalāl)
s.f. Legitimate issue from both sides (paternal and maternal)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوْلَاد (awlād, “offspring, child(ren), descendant”), from Arabic أَوْلَاد (ʔawlād, “children”), plural of وَلَد (walad, “child; son”).

def: Female issue. aulād rishta-i-mustaqīma .; H. A. ek bāp kī aulād . Lineal descendants. aulād-i-zakūr ; H. beṭe . Male issue. aulād-i-sahīh-un-nasab, aulād-i-nasabī ; H. sachchī aulād . Legitimate issue. aulād-i-gair-sahih-un-nasab, kam-asal aulād . Illegitimate issue. aulād kī aulād ; H. nātī pote . Grandchildren. aulād kī jaṛ, Fac . The male organ of generation. aulād najīb-ut-tarafain ; H. kulvantī santān . Legitimate issue from both sides, maternal and paternal. bakre kī aulād. Fac . Illegitimate issue.
adj. Of inauspicious or evil omen; — unfortunate; — s.m. Bad omen.
(comp. of اوليٰٰ and the Pers. تر) adj. Better, best.
s.f. Superiority, preëminence, excellence (see aulā).
s. (dakh.) Twins.
s.m. = ail-fail, q.v. s.m. Abusive language, obscenity; prating, chatter, babble (also aul-faul: used by Mohammadan women).
more: Mah. Wom. n. m. Cor. of A. al the, fel act. 1. Abusive language. 2. Babble; blab; prate; gabble; tattle.

def: s.m. Friends (of God), saints, holy men, prophets, apostles; a saint ( = walī); the holy; — (iron.) a godless person.
more: s. plur. (of ولي) 1. Friends, companions (especially, of the prophets). 2. The saints, the apostles, the holy. اوليأالله auliyā-i-allāh, Friends of God.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوْلِیَاء (awliyā'), from Arabic أَوْلِيَاء (ʔawliyāʔ).

def: adj. Strange, odd, singular, unusual; unpleasant, wearisome, dull; uneasy, restless, perturbed; — s.m. Strangeness, oddness, newness; unpleasantness, dullness, irksomeness; uneasiness, restlessness.
more: adj. au bad, lakhnā to see. 1. New; unused to; strange; queer. Makān aulū sā lagtā hai . 2. Unusual; unaccustomed; unwonted; singular. 3. Unpleasant; irksome; tedious; heavy. 4. Disquieted; restless; uneasy.
n. m. 1. Strangeness; newness. 2. Unpleasantness; irksomeness. 3. Uneasiness; restlessness.

def: s.m. Aid, help, assistance; — aider, helper; defender, protector, guard.
more: s. m. Aid, assistance, a defender, an aider.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Deep ( = gahrā); sunk.
s.f. Depth.
n. f. Depth ( gahrāī ).
adj. (f. -ī), With the face down; upset, upside down, overturned, inverted, topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, ruined; adverse (as fortune); crooked, distorted; — s.m. Blockhead, fool
more: adj. H. oṅ down, S. धा to place. 1. Inverted; inverse; upside down; topsy turvy; overturned; adverse. Auṅdhā nasīb, phūṭe karm . Prov. saying. Upturned my fate and cracked my pate. 2. Crooked; distorted; wrong; perverse. auṅdhī peshānī, yā khoprī , n. f. See ulṭī khoprī. auṅdhe muṅh dūdh pīnā , v. n. lit . To suck with the head down. To be babyish, childish, ignorant. Phr. Not to know a hawk from a handsaw. Tum to kuchh jānte hī nāhīṅ, auṅdhe muṅh dūd pīte ho! You know nothing, you're a sucking baby, taking milk with your head down. auṅdhe muṅh gir'nā , v. n. To fall on one's face, or belly. Auṅdhe muṅh, Shaitān kā dhakkā ! Prov. Fallen on your face, it's a shove from the devil! auṅdhe honā , v. n. To be tipsy or drunk; to get under the table. Ek hī piyālī meṅ ho gayā auṅdhā .

adv. Turned over; upside down; inside out; heels over head. Auṅdhā chale, auṅdho batlāve, auṅdho jag meṅ āë, Kahe Kabīr, auṅdhī kartūti sīdhā huā nā jāë . Kabīr. Crooked ways and crooked speech, and upside down brought forth, Thus saith Kabīr, all crooked deeds shall crooked be henceforth.

n. m. 1. A fool; an idiot; simpleton; blockhead. 2. ( Slang ). A catamite. Auṅdhā khāë lauṅdā . Prov. auṅdhe kā jan'ā , Wom. n. m. Son of an idiot; a great fool. (An abuse). auṅdhā leṭnā, auṅdhā sīdhā —, v. n. To lie on one's face or belly. Auṅdhā sīdhā na leṭ, koī kal be-kal ho-jāëgī . Wom.
adj. Upside down, overturned. اوندهابخت aundhā baḵẖt, s. m. Misfortune, adverse fortune. اوندهي پيشاني aundhī peshānī, adj. Shortsighted, unfortunate.
subs: v.n. To lie on the face
adj. Crooked-headed; ill-fated, unlucky, unfortunate; shortsighted
adv. On the face, with the face or head down; head-foremost, headlong
lit. 'To suck with the face down'; to act like a child, to be silly, ignorant, &c.

def: v.t. To turn upside down, turn over, upset, invert, overturn, reverse; to pour out (water), empty (a vessel); to hang down (the head)
more: v. a. 1. To turn over; turn upside down; invert. Is kūṅḍe ko auṅdhā do . 2. To pour out; empty. Kyoṅ pānī uṅdhā rahe ho?

def: v.n. To be overturned, to turn upside down, upset; to be poured out; to lower (as the sky).
more: v. a. To turn upside down, to reverse, invert, overturn, overset, spill.
v. n. To lower (as the sky).

def: v. a. ( Ṭhagī .) To eat.
adv. More or less.
adv. S. ऊन less, H. paun three quarters. From 25 to one per cent. less. At a loss. Aune paune bech-kar jamā palle bāṅdho. aune paune bech'nā , v. n. To sell at a loss.
adv. At whatever can be got, at a loss.
s.f. Silence, dumbness.
s.f. & adv. Roving about, rambling, sauntering.
s.m. = آنولا āṅʼolā, q.v.
v.t. To boil, &c. = auṭānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to boil; to boil, boil down; to heat.
s.f. = auṭāʼonī, q.v. s.f. An earthen vessel in which milk is boiled.
v.n. To be boiled, &c.
more: v. n. Same as اوٿنا auṭnā, q. v.

def: v. n. 1. To be boiled (any liquid). 2. To consume with rage or vexation. Tere lachchhanoṅ se maiṅ rāt din auṅṭūṅ hūṅ . Wom.
adj. And, also, for the rest, besides, again, moreover; but, yet, still; over, else; and lo!; — another, other, different; more, additional
more: adj. S. अपर Pr. avar another. 1. Different; other; not the same; foreign. Ham khūb samajhte haiṅ terī tarz-i-nigah ko, Hai qahr kī āṅkh aur, muhabbat kī nazar aur . Dā/?/g. 2. Only; no more; only that. Dunyā hai, aur matlab . Prov. Self and the world is all. 3. More; greater; worse. Saut par saut, aur jalāpā . Prov. Another and another co-wife yet, so burning coals on coals of fire are set.

conj. 1. And; else; besides; also; likewise. 2. But; however; yet; still. 3. Than (Correlative of 'no sooner'). Chiknā ghaṛā hai, būṅd paṛī aur ḍhal gaī . Naghat. A greasy jar, the drop slides off no sooner fallen.

n. m. 1. Any one else; some body else. Mujhe aur, na tujhe ṭhaur . Prov. For me no other, for you no whither. (After a quarrel, the wife to her husband —"I may not take another husband, and you could not be happy elsewhere.") 2. Others; other people. Aur ko mile purakh man-māne, Mujhe mile nādāne! Wom. Song. Lāonī . Nice husbands others to their mind have found, Me wretched to this senseless boy fast bound. aur irādah , n. m. 1. A different or lurking design. 2. A double entendre. Har chand havas ne to kiyā aur irādah, Par rah gaë kuchh soch-ke woh māre hayā ke . Madārī Lāl. Howe'er the lust of sense a further purpose had, Yet on reflection its fulfilment shame forbad. aur bhī . And; more; still; more; also; moreover. aur to aur , adv. Moreover; besides that; put the rest aside. Aur to aur, sage bāp kī to māntā hī nahīṅ . Wom. Others apart, he wont even mind his own father. aur to kyā , …

conj. And, also. adj. More, other, else. اورايک aur ek, Another, separate, distinct, else. اورکيا aur kyā, What else? certainly. اورنهين تو aur nahīṅ to, And if not then, and otherwise. اورهي aur hī, Quite different, extraordinary.
subs: Others, others besides; other kinds of
And again, and more, still more, also, moreover
Others apart, others being put aside
All other, all the others, the rest
One word more; — only hear! just listen!
Anyone else, any other, another, different ( = koʼī aur)
Anything else, some other, some or something more; something different ( = kućh aur)
What else? what more? what next? — certainly, assuredly, of course
And if not then; and otherwise
And this that; moreover, besides that.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀯𑀭 (avara), from Sanskrit अपर (ápara, “other, different”). Cognate with Punjabi ہوْر (hor).

def: s.m. pl. (of wird), Daily rehearsals (of the Qǒrʼān), commemorations, devotional exercises.
more: s. plur. (of ورد wird ) Daily rehearsals, commemorations, devotion.

def: s.m. pl. (of waraq), Leaves (of a tree, &c.); pages.
s.m. A throne; — places where goods are manufactured for sale ( = S. avaḍranga)
subs: lit. 'Ornament of the throne'; name of an emperor of Hindūstūn (who died in 1707)
s.f. A kind of cloth; — a kind of eruption (said to have broken out in the reign of Aurangzeb)

def: s. m. A kind of silk so called.
a male given name, Aurangzeb, from Persian
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَورَنْگزیب (awrang-zēb, literally “Ornament of the Throne”).

def: n. m. 1. A kind of cloth. 2. A boil, or eruption said to have broken out in the reign of Aurangzeb.
more: s. m. A kind of cloth so called.

def: s.m.f. Legitimate, legitimately born; — a legitimate child (i.e. by a wife of the same tribe).
adv. All round, about, around; lengthwise and breadthwise; — adj. Level, even ( = ćauras, q.v.).
more: adv. All around. auras chauras , adj. Level; even; smooth.
s. m. (dakh.) 1. Environs, confines; circumference, all about or around: see اطراف at̤rāf. 2. Breadth and length. 3. Capacity (of a vessel: Harris ).

ety: for A. عورة, v.n. fr. عور 'to be blind,' &c.
def: s.f. (orig.) The private part or parts (so called because it is abominable to uncover or expose them); — (in Urdū) a woman; a wife
more: n. f. lit . naked. A female (استري). Aurat par hāth uṭhānā achchhā nahīṅ . Prov. Saying. It is not right to lift one's hand on a woman. aurāt tārak-ul-dunyā ; H. ārjāṅ, jatī . A nun. aurat kī zāt ; Hin. aurat-bānī ; Rus. baiyar-bānī ; Mah. mastūrāt , n. f. The female sex; womankind. aurat-i-hāmila ; H. garabhvantī istrī , A pregnant woman. aurat-i-zī-rutbah . G. G. A woman of rank. aurat kā bhagā le jānā . G. G. Abduction. aurat kā rāj , Woman's reign or supremacy.
(Heb. from Heb. To blind, to strip naked, or To be naked) s. f. 1. The pudenda of man or woman, that which is concealed through modesty. 2. (in Persian and Hindī) A woman, a wife.
ex: یہ ہم گنہ گار عورتیں ہیں جو اہل جبہ کی تمکنت سے نہ رعب کھائیں نہ جان بیچیں نہ سر جھکائیں نہ ہاتھ جوڑیں
ye ham gunahgār aurtẽ ha͠i jo ahl-e-jubbā kī tamaknat se na rob khāẽ na jān becẽ na sar jhukāẽ na hāth joṛẽ
It is we sinful women who are not awed by the grandeur of those who wear gowns, who don't sell our lives, who don't bow our heads, who don't fold our hands together
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَوْرَت (awrat), from Arabic عَوْرَة (ʕawra, “genitals; nudity”), where the shift to "woman" in early New Indo-Aryan happened because it was considered immodest to expose the female body in public. First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi عَورَت ('vrt)/औरत (aurata).

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻaurat), Private parts, pudenda; women (the com. signification in Urdū).
more: s. f. plur. (of عورت in Persian and Hindī) Women.

ety: ʻaurat, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Of or relating to woman; like a woman.
direct plural of عَوْرَت (ʻaurat, “woman”)
vocative plural of عَوْرَت (ʻaurat, “woman”)
oblique plural of عَوْرَت (ʻaurat, “woman”)
s.m. Any rite performed in honour of the dead; obsequies; funeral solemnities.
adj. Crooked, deformed, badly-formed, ugly; inverted, transposed; — s.m. Crookedness, deceit ( = fareb)
adj. Mad, crazy, &c. = bāʼolā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Mad, crazy, deranged, demented, insane.
oblique plural of اَور (aur, “other”)
s.m. Submarine fire.
s.m. Rice sown in April-May and growing luxuriantly during the rainy season.
E. & W. n. m. S. अश् to eat. Rice sown in Chait or Bai-sākh (Aug. Sep.) and growing rapidly through the rainy season.
s.m. Noble properties or attributes, praiseworthy qualifications, &c.
more: s. plur. (of وصف) Praises, qualities, endowments, descriptions. اوصاف حميده auṣāf-i-ḥamīda, adj. Praiseworthy qualifications, noble properties.

def: n. f. Rus. Illness (بيماری).
s.m. Calmness, coolness, self-possession, presence of mind, courage; sense, senses; sensation
more: n. m. from sān sharpness. Sensibility; sense ( havās ); presence of mind. Ausān se bāt karo. ausān uṛ jānā, khatā honā, yā jāte rahnā , v. n. To be stunned, stupefied, at a loss; to stand aghast; lose one's senses. Ausān khatā hue, aql chakkar meṅ āī . The senses at fault, the judgment in a whirl.
s. m. 1. Sensation, sense. 2. Courage. 3. Presence of mind.
subs: ausān jāte-rahnā, To lose (one's) presence of mind, to lose (one's) senses, to be stupefied, to stand aghast

def: adj. Middle, middling, intervening, intermediate; average; — s.m. (f.?), Middle, medium, mean, average interval; mediocrity
more: (from وسط) 1. adj. Middle, middling, intervening. 2. s. m. The middle, medium, mean, interval. 3. Mediocrity, moderation.

def: adj. Midmost, middling, moderate; pertaining to the average.
s.f. Care, solicitude, anxiety. (Also written अवसेर.)
s.f. Care, solicitude, anxiety. (Also written अवसेर.)
s.f. Medicine, medicament, drug, any herb, mineral, &c. used in medicine
s. f. A medicament, a drug, any herb, mineral, &c. used in medicine.
adj. Medicinal; — s.m. Ingredient of a medicine; materia medica.
s.f. Secretion or rheum of the eyes.
s.m. pl. (of waṣī), Executors, administrators of the divine will, or of the will of mortals, legatees; preceptors, guardians.
more: s. plur. (of وصي) Executors, administrators (of the divine will, or of the wills of mortals).

ety: aus˚ = Prk. ओवस(इ) or अववस(इ) = S. अपवस(ति), rt. अप+वस्
def: v.n. To become musty; sticky, &c. = ubasnā, q.v. v.n. To give forth a smell, become fetid; to turn; to smell offensive (meat or food); to rot, putrefy, become rancid or rank; to become mouldy, musty, or mildewed; to ferment; to become sticky or viscous; to become stale, or useless; to be weary, to be out of patience.
more: W. v. n. To become sticky. See ubasnā (1. 2.)
Mah. v. a. To knead the flour.
v. n. To become musty, to rot, to putrefy.

def: on average
adj. Without consideration, thoughtless, heedless, unconcerned.
s.m. Defunct person ( = pret); spirit of a deceased person; one who dies without leaving issue; an unmarried man; stupid fellow, dolt, blockhead, fool ( = mūrkh).
more: Hin. Wom. ( Contemptuously ) ūṭ'ṛā, ūti'yā, ut'tā; Old H. aūt; Gaṛh autā , n. m. S. अपुत्र from अ not, पुत्र a son. 1. One who dies unmarried; a batchelor. 2. A stupid person; a blockhead; a fool. 1. Bāman kā ūt . 2. Bārā muṭṭhī kā ūt . 3 Chālīs-serā ūt. ūt-pit'tar , n. m. The spirits of the dead. Is ko kisī ūt pittar kī ho gaī hai . Hin. ūt-paṭāṅg . 1. Nonsense. See aṛaṅg taṛaṅg (1). 2. Harsh, hard, bitter words. Ūt patāṅg beloge tau achchhā na hogā . 3. Words for which it is difficult to find a rhyme. Maiṅ ne kyā is gazal ko sahl kiyā, Qāfye hī the is ke ūt paṭāṅg . Mīr.
(perhaps, for s. अपुत्र ) s. m. 1. One who dies without leaving issue. 2. An unmarried man. 3. (in Hindī phraseology) A stupid person, blockhead, dunce.

def: s.m. pl. (of watad), Pegs, pins; props; chiefs, nobles; certain four saints (regarded as the props of the faith).
more: s. plur. (of وتد) Pegs; nobles chiefs.

def: s.m. Boiling; evaporation by boiling; — a knife used for cutting tobacco.
more: 1. verbal n. (from اوٿنا auṭnā ) Boiling, evaporation by boiling. 2. s. m. A knife used for cutting tobacco.

def: v. a. To boil; boil down.

ety: caus. of auṭnā or auṅṭnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To cause to boil; to boil, boil down; to heat.
more: v. a. (caus. of اوٿنا) To cause to boil, to make hot.

def: s.f. An earthen vessel in which milk is boiled.
n. f. An earthen vessel for boiling milk.
s.m. Excellence, superiority.
s.m. Excellence, superiority.

ety: auṭ˚ = Prk. ओउट्ट(इ) = S. अवकुट्य(ति), rt. अव+कुट्
def: v.t. To boil; to evaporate over a fire; — v.n. To boil; to be hot; to be consumed with rage or vexation. (See auṅṭnā.)
more: 1. v. a. To boil (as milk), to evaporate over a fire. 2. v. n. To consume with rage or vexation.

def: s.m. Anxiety, disquietude, perturbation, uneasiness, regret; ardent desire, longing, eagerness, impatience.
s.m. Anxiety, disquietude, perturbation, uneasiness, regret; ardent desire, longing, eagerness, impatience.

ety: inf. n. fr. عوذ 'to seek protection,' &c.
def: s.m. Seeking (God's) protection; fleeing to for refuge; refuge, asylum.
more: (Heb. ) s. m. Refuge, asylum, fleeing to God for protection.

def: s.f. pl. (of وضع q.v.), Behaviour, manners, deportment, address, breeding, politeness; gestures, postures: — auẓāʻ-at̤wār = auẓāʻ.
more: s. plur. (of وضع) Behaviour, manners, breeding, politeness, &c.

def: s.m. pl. (of wazn), Weights, measures, metres.
(pl. of وزر), s.m. sing. & plur. Tool, instrument; arms, tools, instruments, apparatus, &c. = ālāt, q.v.
more: n. m. Pl. of وزر Tools. See آلات.
s. plur. (of وزر) 1. Sins or offences (but in Hindī not used in this sense). 2. Arms, tools, apparatus. 3. The rigging and implements of a ship or boat. 4. Asylums, places of defence.

def: s.m. Potter's kiln; kiln; furnace.
more: n. m. A potter's kiln ( bhaṭṭī ). 1. Māṅ kā peṭ kumhār kā āvā, koī kālā, koī gorā re . Prov. The mother's womb is as a potter's kiln, Pots black and fair both issue from within. 2. Ek āve ke bartan haiṅ . Prov. Chips of the same block. 3. Kisī kā āvā bigṛe, inkā khadāne kā khadānā bigaṛ gayā . Prov. Some potters find their kilns go wrong; for him the clay-pit has gone wrong.
s. m. (dakh.) A furnace or receptacle for melting (glass), a kiln (for bricks, &c.: Harris ).

def: s.m. A kiln, &c. = āwā, q.v. A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)
A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)
more: prep. From down from, off, away. It is the reverse of आ , and is sometimes used to denote deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.

ety: perf. part. of āʼonā = ānā, 'to come'
def: s.m. Coming, approach
subs: s.m. = āwā-jāwī, s.f. = āwā-jāʼī, s.f. Coming and going; intercourse, communication; visiting, interchange of visits; access; dancing attendance (upon).

def: s.m. Cover, covering.
s.m. Boundary, limit; distinction.
adj. Not to be spoken of; vile, bad; — silent, dumb.
corr. from ābādān, q.v.
s.f. = ābādānī, q.v. s.f. A habitation; a cultivated, populous, or pleasant place; population; cultivation; abundance; prosperity; advancement; civilization; enjoyment, pleasure.
v.t. To forsake ( = tyāg karnā).
prop. n. The province of Oude.
s. f. 1. Agreement, engagement. 2. Time, period. 3. Limit. 4. (for अयोध्या ) A name of the province of Oude. 5. (for अवध्य ) adj. Sacred, inviolable.
adj. Attentive, careful, cautious; — s.m. Attention, care, caution, vigilance; staring at.
more: adj. Cautious, attentive. s. m. Caution, attention.

def: s.m. Compassion towards the lowly.
s.m. Ascertainment; affirmation, emphasis; stating or holding positively; accurate determination; limitation, restriction (as to sense, instances, &c.).
s.f. Unobstructedness.
adv. As far as; as long as.
s.m. A kind of religious mendicant (see abdhūt).
more: s. m. A class of faḳīrs.

def: adj. Not to be killed, inviolable; not fit to be killed.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Gūjarātī Brāhmans.
more: s. m. Name of a tribe of Gujrātī Brāhmans.

def: s.m. Bathing, ablution, dip, dive, plunge.
s.m. Bathing, ablution, dip, dive, plunge.
s.m. Coming and going; intercourse, &c. ( = ānā-jānā, āwā-jāʼā, &c.); ingress and egress; being born and dying; transmigration.
more: (from آنا To come, q. v. and गमन Going) s. Coming and going.

def: part. Known, understood, comprehended.
s.f. Perception, comprehension, knowledge.
s.m. Coming and going; intercourse, &c. ( = ānā-jānā, āwā-jāʼā, &c.); ingress and egress; being born and dying; transmigration.
more: n. f. H. ānā to come, S. गम् to go. 1. Coming and going; ingress and egress ( ānā jānā ). Nit ke āvā-gavan te nipat an-ādar hoë, Yā te nit nit par-graha bhūli jāū mat koë . Doha. From constant visiting esteem doth fall away. This maxin bear in mind, nor daily visits pay. 2. Transmigration of souls ( jīvan-maran ).

def: part. adj. Detested; blamed, reproached; wicked, vile; — s.m. Blame, reproach, obloquy; satire.
s.m. Defect, blemish, fault, demerit, worthlessness, evil, vice; harm, mischief; contempt, disregard ( = S. avagan); a corrupt or vicious person ( = augunī)
more: E; n. m. S. अव off, away, गुण quality. 1. Want of goodness or virtue; demerit; badness. Champā, to meṅ tīn gun raṅg, rūp aru bās; Augun tujh meṅ kaun sā, bhaṅvar na baiṭhe pās . Dohā. Fair form, rich color, and sweet scent, Champā , three merits thine, What then thy fault, the beetle woos thee not— all too supine. 2. Vice; wickedness; evil; evil deeds. 1. Mere augan chit na dharo re . Bhajan. Regard not, O Lord, the evil that is in me! 2. Gun sīkh-ke, augan sīkho . Prov. He learns good and does evil. 3. Defect; fault; flaw; failing; blemish. Mujh meṅ kyā augan hai? 4. Immorality; looseness of morals; impropriety; sin. Augan nandī ko chhailā sikhābat hai, Khet pe le jāt saṅg merī nand ko, Chhailā toṛ-ke bhauṅṭā chabābat hai . Rus. Song. This rake corrupts my husband's sister, to The field he takes her with him thereunto, An Indian corn he gives to her to chew. 5. Disease; disorder; malady…
subs: (-ko), To impute a fault, vice, &c. (to), to slander, defame.

def: s.f. Disregard, disrespect, contempt, despite
subs: adj. Contemptuous, degrading, derogatory; — s.m. A contemptuous person, &c.

def: s.m. Sending for, calling, summoning, inviting; call, summons; invitation.
s. f. Report.
more: 1. s. f. (from آنا To come) Approach, advance. 2. A saddlecloth adorned with fringes, &c.

def: n. f. 1. Report; rumour; gossip; hearsay; false rumour or report. 2. News of one's arrival, coming or advance ( avātī ). avāī uṛānā , v. a. To spread a false report.
s.f. Approach, advance; proximity; arrival; report or news of (one's) approach or arrival ( = āvātī); report, rumour, hearsay ( = afwāh); — a saddle-cloth adorned with fringes; — a kind of pickaxe.
s.f. Approach, advance; proximity; arrival; report or news of (one's) approach or arrival ( = āvātī); report, rumour, hearsay ( = afwāh); — a saddle-cloth adorned with fringes; — a kind of pickaxe.
s.m. pl. (of ʻaib, q.v.), Blemishes, defects; faults, vices.
s. m. f. plur. (of اوّل) Commencements, beginning.
s.m. (f. ?), Beginnings; beginning, first part, early portion or period; the first ten days of a lunar month (the remaining decades are termed awāsit and awāḵẖir, q.q.v.)
subs: awāʼil-ʻumr, s.f. Early youth, tender age, child hood; — s.m. A youth
adv. In the beginning at first.

def: adj. (f. awaiʼī), Future, yet to come
s. f. Coming and going, intercourse, dancing after.
s.m. Insurance (syn. bīmā).
part. Ascertained, concluded, certain.
s.m. Leisure, opportunity ( = ausar); interval, space, room, way, scope.
more: s. m. 1. Leisure, opportunity. 2. Interval, space.

def: adj. (f. inī), Unfruitful, barren.
s.m. plur. (of āḵẖir), Ends, latter parts, extremes; the last ten days of a lunar month.
more: s. plur. (of آخر) Ends, latter parts.

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻāqibat, q.v.), Ends, &c.; consequences; — posterity.
s.m. Enclosed space formed by a cluster of peasant's houses; a string fastened round the wheel of a distaff.
more: E. n. f. 1. An enclosed space formed by a cluster of rāyat's houses. 2. A string fastened round the wheel of a distaff ( jatnī , W).

def: adj. Of inauspicious or evil omen; — unfortunate; — s.m. Bad omen.
adj. Hanging down, depending, hanging on or upon; — s.m. Asylum, protection, dependence, support.
s.m. Depending (upon), supporting oneself (upon); dependence, reliance; support, prop.
— see اولمب avalamb.
adj. Depending or hanging (on), dependent (on); delaying, dilatory; — s.m. Dependent; refugee; procrastinator.
part. adj. Suspended (from), depending (on), trusting (to), supported (by); protected, cherished; alighting, descending.
s.f. Row, range, continuous line, series; lineage, dynasty
more: n. f. 1. Misfortune; disaster; misery. 2. Mortal danger. See alī .

def: s.m. Looking at, beholding; sight, view, inspection.
part. Seen, looked at, inspected; — s.m. A saint of the Jain sect.
v.t. To look at, see, inspect, view.
s.m. Leaping suddenly (upon), breaking forth, rushing on or away
s.m. Debt, loan; — colour (see wām).
more: s. Debt, loan: colour: see وام wām.

def: adj. Undermost, inferior, lowest, base, vile, low; growing less, decreasing; — s.m. A lunar day exactly coinciding with a solar one.
public; popular
s.m. pl. (of A. عامة ʻāmmat, fr. ʻām, q.v.), The commonalty, the common people, the common sort, the vulgar, the populace
more: n. m. pl. of عامه 1. The common people; the mass; populace; vulgar. 2. The scum or dregs of society. avām-un-nās ; H. sab log , The people; the community; the population; the public. avām kī ittilā ke līye mushtahar hotā hai . Is hereby promulgated for general information. avām ke fāëde ke līye . For the public good or welfare. avām ke līye . For the public; common.
s. plur. (of عامّة) The vulgar, populace, common people. عوام الناس aﻌwāmmu-n-nās, The vulgar, common people.
subs: The common people, the populace at large, the public.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عوام ('awām), from Arabic عَوَامّ (ʕawāmm), the plural of عَامَّة (ʕāmma). Compare Punjabi ਅਵਾਮ (avām).

def: common people
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَوَامُّالنَّاس (awāmmu-n-nās), from Arabic عَوَامّ النَّاس (ʕawāmm an-nās).

def: s.m. Kiln = āwā, q.v. A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)
s.m. Coming, approach, &c. ( = awāʼī, q.v.)
more: n. m. W. The iron ring round the nave.
(see آنا) s. m. Coming, approach. آون آون āwan āwan, s. f. Tidings of arrival.
subs: s.m. Tidings or news of arrival.

def: s.m. The field into which rice has been transplanted (cf. āman).
s.m. Iron band round the navs of a wheel.
n. m. ānā to come. Tidings or news of arrival.
s.f. plur. (of ān, 'time'), Times, seasons.
s.m. pl. (of عون ʻaun), Aids, helps; assistants, aiders.
(in Braj) v. n. To come.
s.m. Vessel, pan.
more: (comp. of آب and وند) s. m. A vessel, a pan.

def: see s.v. āwanā or āʼonā.
s.f. The earth, world; — avanī-pa, s.m. Lord of the earth (an epithet of a king or ruler).
more: pron. (dakh.) They, them, to them, he, she, it.

def: s.f. Name of a city, the modern Oujain.
s.f. Name of a city, the modern Oujain.
more: s. f. Name of a city, the modern Oujein.

def: s.f. Delay; — adv. Late, &c. = aver, q.v. s.f. Delay, &c.; — adv. Late, &c. (see aber)
subs: awerī-sawerī, adv. Late and early, at all hours, unseasonably; late or early, late in the evening or early in the morning
v.n. To delay, loiter, dally, to procrastinate.

def: s.m. Account-book, &c. = āmār and avārija, q.q.v.; also = āwāra, q.v. s.m. Heap, pile. (The word would appear to be peculiar to the Panjāb.)
s.m. Injustice, tyranny ( = S. अपकार).
s.m. Injustice, tyranny ( = S. अपकार).

r: āwar, āwardan, ā, war, bar, zor-āwar
ety: rt. of āwardan; ā+war = Zend bar = S. भर्], Bringing, bearing; possessing, endowed with (used as last member of compounds, e.g. zor-āwar
def: adj. Having strength, strong, powerful).
more: part. act. (from آوردن) in compos. Bringing, bearing, bringer, bearer, producing.

def: adj. Low, inferior, hinder; — s.m. Low person; a śūdra.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀯𑀭 (avara), from Sanskrit अपर (ápara, “other, different”). Cognate with Punjabi ہوْر (hor).

def: adj. Separated from one's family ( = judā); without house and home; wandering, roving; astray; abandoned, lost; dissolute; — s.m. Wanderer; vagabond; profligate
more: adj. 1. Roving; wandering about; separated from one's family. 2. Abandoned; lost; destitute of name or character. āvārah phir'nā , Lurking. G. G. To stroll about; wander up and down. Kharāb khastā, nāj sastā, āvarah phirtā hai . Prov. saying. Ruined and lost he is tossed about like cheap grain. āvārah kar'nā , v. a. To make a vagabond of; to spoil; corrupt (kharāb k.). āvārah honā , v. n. The verbal form of اَواره q. v.

adj. Vagabond, wanderer, dissolute. آواره هونا āwāra honā, To wander, to be distressed, to be oppressed, to be miserable. آواره کرنا āwāra karnā, To make a wanderer, to expel, to harass. آواره گردي āwāra-gard-ī, s. f. Vagrancy, wandering about. (Plur. آوارگان).
subs: To wander or rove about; to wander astray; to go loafing about
(-ko), To drive out of house and home; to make a vagabond of; to ruin
To be separated from one's family, &c. (see āwāra).

def: s.f. Wandering; vagrancy; profligacy.
more: s. f. Wandering, the state of a wanderer, vagrancy, profligacy.

def: Awaran (a city in Awaran district, Balochistan, Pakistan)
s.m. Covering; a covering, shield, protection; obstruction, lock.
adj. Not natural, artificial, false; affected; assumed (opp. to āmad, 'that which comes natural').
more: adj. lit . brought. Opp. of āmad what comes naturally. Artificial; affected; unnatural; false; far-fetched.

def: s.f. Period of life, &c. = āyurdī, q.v.
more: s. f. The allotted period of any oneʼs life, a life time, an age.

ety: perf. part. of āwardan; see āwar
def: s.m. That which is brought or carried over
more: n. lit . That which is brought over. One who is favored; a protogè.
part. pass. (of آوردن) Brought. (Sans. आ and ऊढ Borne, from r. वहु Bear).
subs: one who is taken into favour
s.m. Registrar or writer of accounts as delivered.

def: adj. (in Gram.), Not classified.
s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
more: n. m. 1. A day book ( roz-namchah ); diary; journal. 2. Abstract account of collections and remittances; a book of receipts and disbursements ( jama-kharch bahī ).

def: s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
more: s. m. A journal, a diary.

def: s.m. pl. of ʻāriẓ, or ʻāriẓa, qq.v.
s.m. = āwārija, q.v. s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
s.m. Stoppage, obstruction, hindrance, resistance, restraint, confinement; — the seraglio of a palace, the queen's apartment
s.m. Absence of impediment, non-opposition, assent, concurrence; absence of contention, tranquillity, quiet.
s.m. Turning; turn; curl; whirlpool, eddy.
part. Hindered, restrained, stopped, obstructed, kept in order, controlled; besieged, imprisoned, shut up, confined, secluded.
s.m. House, dwelling, habitation.
adv. Certainly, &c. = avaśya, q.v. adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
adj. Unsubmissive to another's will, not under control, unsubdued, unrestrained, disobedient; — not having one's own free will, subject to another, without choice, helpless, powerless.
s.m. End, conclusion, completion; boundary, limit; death, destruction.
s.m. Occasion, fit moment, time or season (of), favourable opportunity; vacation, leisure
subs: To find occasion, get an opportunity; to have or find leisure
To watch for an opportunity, to observe the time or season (of); to bide (one's) time.

def: s.m. Residue, &c. = avaśesh, q.v.
s.m. Residue, remainder, surplus; end, close, termination, appendix, supplement (syn. tatimma).
s.f. Unripe corn (chiefly barley) cut from time to time and brought home to be eaten.
more: Bhoj. n. f. Unripe corn cut from time to time and brought home to be eaten without being threshed.

def: = avaśya, q.v. adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀑𑀲𑀸 (osā), from Sanskrit अवश्या (avaśyā). Cognate with Bengali ওস (ōs).

def: part. = ava-śishṭ.
part. Left, remaining.
s.m. pl. (of ausat̤), Middles, means, averages; the period of a lunar month between the tenth and twentieth days.
s.f. State, condition, circumstances ( = ḥāl); age; life; story, narrative (of a life).
more: n. f. S. अव well, स्था to stand. 1. State; condition; age. 2. Period; duration; life ( ārbal ). Yeh itnī hī austhā le-kar ayā thā . 3. Story; narrative; history. Rājā ne sārī avasthā kahī .
s. f. State, condition (of humanity varying with the progress of time, as youth, age, &c.).

def: adj. Unreal, unsubstantial, vain, trifling; unessential, not belonging to the true nature of a thing; — s.m. A worthless thing, nothing, &c.
s.f. Unreality, insubstantiality; non-essentialness.
adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
s.m. Anything necessary, necessity, expediency; — adj. Necessary, indispensable, inevitable, actual.
s.m. Anything necessary, necessity, expediency; — adj. Necessary, indispensable, inevitable, actual.
s.f. Necessity, need, indispensableness, inevitability.
necessity, need, inevitability
s.m. Crest; ear-ring; ornament, gem; wealth; halter, rein.

s.m. Descent (especially of a deity from heaven), appearance of any deity upon earth; incarnation (more particularly of Vishṇu in ten principal forms, viz. 1˚ Matsya, 'the fish'; 2˚ Kaććhapa, 'the tortoise'; 3˚ Varāha, 'the boar'; 4˚ Nr̤i-singha, 'the man-lion'; 5˚ Vāmana, 'the dwarf'; 6˚ Paraśu-rāma; 7˚ Rāma; 8˚ Balarāma, or Kr̤ishna; 9˚ Buddhā; 10˚ Kalkī); — any new and unexpected appearance or phenomenon; any pious or distinguished person (in the language of respect or flattery); a wicked, depraved or quarrelsome person (ironically); — crossing ( = utār); translation; a tīrth or sacred place of Hindū pilgrimage
more: n. m. S. अवतार from अवतृ to descend, Pr. oāra . 1. An incarnation of the deity. ☞ The Hindūs reckon ten incarnations of the god Vishnu:—1st, Machh a fish; 2nd, Kachh a tortoise; 3rd, Bārāh a boar; 4th, Nar-Singh a man-lion; 5th, Baunā or Bāman a dwarf; 6th; Paras Rām ; 7th, Rām; 8th, Krishn; 9th, Budh; 10th, Kalkī or Niskalank , which is yet to appear on the culmination of the kal-yug the iron age or age of sin. 2. A pious or distinguished person, in the language of flattery, is also called an autār . 3. ( Ironic ). A bad, wicked, or quarrelsome person; a wrangling jade ( uṭār ). 4. A mighty, powerful person; a powerful king or authority. Tape rāj aṅgrez kā, jāne jag, sansār, Yā kal-jug ke bīch meṅ, yeh bhī baṛe autār . Dohā. Bright shines the English rule, as all the world doth know, An incarnation great this iron age doth show. autār dhārnā, yā lenā , v. n. To become incarnate; to come…

s. m. Descent, especially of a deity from heaven; incarnation. (The Hindūs reckon the following ten avatārs of the God Vish̤ṇu: 1. मत्स्य matsya, A fish: 2. कच्छप kachchhapa, A tortoise: 3. वराह varāha, A hog: 4. नृसिंह nr̤i-singha, A man-lion: 5. वामन vāmana, A dwarf: 6. परशुराम parasurāma: 7. राम rāma: 8. बलराम balarāma: 9. बुद्ध buddha: 10. कल्की kalkī ).
subs: autār lenā, v.n. To become incarnate, to appear in the world (a deity).

def: v.n. To descend (especially as an incarnation of the deity = autār lenā); to cross, &c. = utarnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To descend (as an incarnation of the Deity, &c.) on earth.

def: = avatarnā, q.v.
s.f. = awātī, q.v. s.f. Approach, coming; advance; coming of an army; approach of a friend; season in which merchandise is expected (cf. awāʼī).

n. f. 1. Coming; approach; advance. Lag rahī hai avātī basant kī, āī na pī kī pātī ho! Song. Basant . The Spring is almost come, no letter from my husband still! 2. News of one's coming; tidings; aviso. āë din , adv. Every day; day after day (nit). Ise to āë din nayā kapṛā chāhiye . + Wom.
s.f. Approach, coming; advance; coming of an army; approach of a friend; season in which merchandise is expected (cf. awāʼī).
more: s. f. Approach, advance; the coming of an army, the approach of a friend; the season in which merchandise is expected.

def: part. Descended; crossed, passed over; translated.
s.f. = ʻaf-ʻaf, q.v. s.f. An imitative sound (as of a dog barking), bow-wow.
more: n. f. An imitative sound, as a bark, bow-vow. av av k ; H. bhauṅ bhauṅ k., bhauṅknā , v. a. To bark.
s. (dakh.) An imitative sound (as, of barking): see عف عف aﻌf aﻌf.

def: n. f. pl. of اول Beginnings. Youth ( chhoṭī umar ). — —, n. m. A youth.
s.m. A limb, member; part, portion; member or component part of a logical argument or syllogism.

ety: Pehl. āfāj, Zend ā+vać, S. वाच्
def: s.f. Sound, noise; voice, tone; whisper; echo; shout, call, cry; report, fame; sentence
more: n. f. 1. Sound; echo; voice; whisper; tone. 2. Call; shout; cry; street cry; mendicant's cry (sadā) . Pukāre hai koī āvāz kas-ke, "Karāṛe, bhurbhure, nībū ke ras ke ." Saīyad. Hear this one in the street cry out, "my gram who'll buy," "Crackling and crisp and lemon-flavored," come and try. 3. Creak; clank; twang; jingle; jar; rustle; tick (of a clock): ringing (of a bell); blast (of a trumpet); report; crash. 4. Note; intonation; tune. 5. Din; roar; uproar; peal; hue and cry; whoop. 6. Reputation; fame; good name. Terī āvāz Makke, Madīne meṅ! Prov. May your voice be heard in Mecca and Medina! (A benediction on one who brings good tidings). āvāz uṭhā nā, baṛhā'nā, yā ūṅchī karnā, v. a . To raise the voice; speak up; to speak in a loud voice; to strain the voice. āvāz ān'ā , v. n. To become audible; to be heard. āvāz bharrā'nā , v. n. To become hoarse and husky. Kul bāje baj-kar ṭūṭ gaë, ā…

s. f. Voice, sound, report, fame, echo, a whisper, sentence. آوازاٿهانا āwāz uṭhānā, To raise the voice, to spread a report. آوازپڙنا āwāz paṛnā, 1. To be reported. 2. The voice to be suppressed (from hoarseness or otherwise).
s. m. (dakh.) Voice, &c. آوازپرکان رکهنا āwāz par kān rakhnā, To listen, to hear or hearken.
subs: To raise the voice, to speak out; to strain the voice; to spread a report
āwāz ūṅćī baṛhānā, To raise the voice ( = āwāz uṭhānā)
āwāz paṛnā, or paṛ-jānā, To become hoarse from cold, singing, &c.; to lose the voice; — āwāz-baiṭhā, adj. Hoarse
or rakhnā, or lagānā, To lend an attentive ear, to hear, listen
(kān-meṅ), To happen to hear, to catch a sound; to overhear; to be heard, to be reported
(-kī), To answer to, or to obey, a call
(-ko), To call out (to), shout (to), to hail; — v.n. To give forth a sound; to answer a call
āwāz-kā ṭappā, s.m. Range or reach of voice; pitch of the voice; hearing-distance, ear-shot
(-kī), To bring forth a sound or report (from), cause to sound; to fire off (a gun, &c.)
To call or cry out (one's wares); to beg; to raise the voice (in singing)
To sing in harmony or concert (with)
To give out a sound; to bring forth or produce a sound; to speak.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آواز (āvāz), from Middle Persian ʾwʾz (āwāz). Cognate with Sanskrit वाच् (vā́c) and Latin vōx. First attested in c. 1665 as Middle Hindi آواز (āvaz).

def: s.m. Noise; loud talk = āwāz; report; rumour, fame
more: s. m. Report, fame, rumour, conversation, talking aloud.
subs: āwāza kasnā, āwāza mārnā (-par), To talk (of), make insinuations (about); to speak in innuendoes; to jeer (at)

def: n. m. Noise. See āvāz (4). Fame; reputation. (Rare).

n. m. H. bolī ṭholī , n. f. Taunts; jeers; inuendoes; insulting expressions. āvāzah kas'nā,—pheknā,—mār'nā , v. n. To speak in inuendoes; speak at; taunt or jeer at; laugh at. Kisī kī bahū beṭī pe āvāzah mat pheko .
direct plural of آواز (āvāz)
oblique plural of آواز (āvāz)
vocative plural of آواز (āvāz)

n. f. H. āo come, S. भक्ति attachment. Courteous welcome (commonly feigned). Jaisī terī āo-bhagat, waisī merī āshirbād . Prov. As your reception, so my adieus. āo-bhagat kar'nā , v. a. To receive a person with due ceremony and respect; to welcome. Sūrat se faqīr thā birogī, Kī āo-bhagat samajh-ke jogī . Nasīm.
s.m. The ocean = abdhi, q.v. s.m. The ocean. — abdhi-kaph, s.m. Cuttle-fish bone.

ety: perf. part. of āweḵẖtan, Zend ā+rt. vij; S. ā+vij or vić
def: part. adj. Suspended, hanging, pendulous, pendent; attached, connected.
s.f. Delay, &c.; — adv. Late, &c. (see aber)
more: adv. Late; too late. See aber. Diye jag-jotī, terī māyal pirove motī, Terī dhannī bichhāve khāṭ, Terī bahnal dekhe bāṭ, Tū sidausī āīyo, averī jāīyo, Jasaudā mātā se Rām, Rām kahiyo . Wom. Song. Diyā-bāṛhāvān . (Sung by Hin. women when putting out the light). O lamp that to the world gives light, Your Ma a string of pearls threads bright, Your wife has spread for you your bed, Your sister waits your footstep's tread, Early come back and late hence flee, Jasodā mother greet for me. aver saver, averī saverī adv. Late or early; late in the evening or early in the morning. aver karnā, der karnā , v. a. To delay; dawdle; loiter; take one's time; put off to the last ( āj kal k . 1.). aver honā , v. n. To be late; to pass away (the time).
subs: awerī-sawerī, adv. Late and early, at all hours, unseasonably; late or early, late in the evening or early in the morning
v.n. To delay, loiter, dally, to procrastinate.

def: part. (of آويختن) Hanging. آويزکرنا āwez karnā, To hang, to suspend.
adj. Hanging, &c. ( = āweḵẖta, q.v.)
subs: (-par), To hang (upon), suspend (a thing, from), attach (to), fix (upon), to post (a notice).

def: s.m. Pendant, ornament for the ear, ear-ring
more: s. m. An earring, drops worn in the ear, a pendant.

def: n. m. An ornament for the ear; an earring.
v. a. P. آويختن to hang. Ped. for H. laṭkānā . To suspend; affix; post (a notice).
adj. Hanging, &c. ( = āweḵẖta, q.v.)
subs: (-par), To hang (upon), suspend (a thing, from), attach (to), fix (upon), to post (a notice).

def: s.f. Hanging, suspension (used in comp.).
s.m. Sun; mountain; preservation; sheep; shawl-goat.
adj. (in law), Indivisible (as property, &c.).
part. adj. Undivided, unseparated; joint; not sharing.
adj. Motionless; unmoved, unshaken, firm, resolute.
s.m. Want of consideration or judgment; injustice. (See a-bićār.)
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Destitute of consideration, wanting in judgment, irreflective; unjust; — an irreflective person; an ill-advised person.
part. Unconsidered, ill-judged, ill-advised, not investigated, undiscussed.
adj. Without interval, close, dense, coarse, uninterrupted.
adj. Not known, not understood.
s.f. Ignorance, stupidity
subs: adj. Unlearned, illiterate, ignorant; — s.m. An illiterate person.

def: adj. Diffusive, pervading, spreading or extending widely.
adj. Not understood, not known, not famous or celebrated.
adj. Unimpaired, perfect, entire; regular, orderly, consistent.
adj. Without change, not liable to change.
adj. Unchangeable, constant, faithful, true; — s.m. One who does not change, a constant person.
adj. Not delaying, prompt, quick, ready, expeditious; — s.m. Haste, despatch, quickness, diligence.
part. adj. Not delaying, &c. = avilamb, q.v. adj. Not delaying, prompt, quick, ready, expeditious; — s.m. Haste, despatch, quickness, diligence.
s.m. Exemption from destruction, exception from loss, safety, perpetuity.
adj. Exempt from liability to destruction or loss, indestructible, imperishable, everlasting, perpetual; safe, entire.
adj. Without proper training, ill-behaved, indecorous; — s.m. Want of good manners or modesty; bad training; rude behaviour, indecorum, impropriety, pertness, wantonness.
adj. Without point or dot.
part. adj. Badly trained, ill-mannered, misbehaving, acting improperly, wicked, vile.
s.f. An immodest or unchaste woman.
adj. Helpless, weak, impotent.
s.f. (of avīr), A childless widow.
adj. Uninterrupted; close, dense; coarse.
Unfolding, developing; development, manifestation; presence, a becoming visible.
part. Unfolded, developed, manifested; apparent.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Not contentious or combative, peaceable, tranquil, quiet: — a peaceable person.
part. adj. Unobstructed, unimpeded; allowed, permitted; compatible; not discordant, harmonious.
adj. Without application; without desire, indifferent; — s.m. An indifferent person, &c.
adv. Without any difference, uniform, alike, like, resembling, undistinguishable, indiscriminate; — s.m. Want of distinction, &c.
part. adj. Not distinguished, not discerned.
adj. Not poisonous; anti-venomous.
s.m. The not being an object of sense; invisibility; — adj. Unacquainted with objects of sense.
adj. Possessed (as by an evil spirit); engrossed (as by a sentiment or feeling).
adj. Mistrusted, distrusted; — s.m. Mistrust, distrust, suspicion, unbelief.
s.m. Distrustfulness, suspiciousness, distrust, &c. = a-viśwās.
adj. & s.m. (f. inī), Wanting in trust, confidence, belief, &c., mistrustful, unfaithful, unbelieving; — a distrustful person; unbeliever, infidel.
s.m. = S اوشوامتا अविश्वासता a-viśwāsatā, s.f. Distrustfulness, suspiciousness, distrust, &c. = a-viśwās.
adj. Not contentious or disputatious, peaceable, conciliatory, gentle; — s.m. A peaceable person, &c.
part. Unmarried, &c. = abi-wāhit, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unmarried, single
subs: a bachelor. — abiwāhitā, s.f. An unmarried woman; a mistress.

def: adj. Without judgment or discrimination ( = avivekī, q.v.); — s.m. Absence of discrimination or judgment, indiscretion, ignorance, stupidity.
s.f. Want of judgment, inconsiderateness, imprudence, &c. ( = avivek).
adj. Undiscriminating, having no judgment, indiscreet, ignorant, superficial, short-sighted; — s.m. An undiscriminating person, &c.
v.n. To come = ānā, q.v.
subs: āwanī-hār, āwane-hār, adj. Coming, having yet to arrive, future; — s.m. One who, or that which, has yet to come ( = āne-hārā).

def: part. Enclosed, surrounded; covered (see next).
s.f. Order, method; returning, repetition; resolution.
adj. First, prior, foremost, chief, greatest, highest, best, excellent; — s.m. The first or earlier part, beginning; — adv. At first, in the beginning, in the first place or instance
more: adj. 1. First; foremost; primus inter pares . 2. Highest; principal; prime; superior ( uttam ). 3. Best; excellent; exquisite; tip-top ( sireshṭh ). 4. Preliminary; preparatory; proemial. avval darjā , n. m. First class; first rate. avval din se , adv. From the first; from the beginning (H. ād se ). avval rahnā , v. n. To stand first in order. 1. gāṛī . 1. conveyance. 2. gaṛī . fixed. 2. okhlī . 1. A mortar. 2. ukhlī . uprooted. avval se ākhir tak , adv. Right through; one's whole generation ( ādse ant tak ). avval manzil , n. f. Funeral rites, or obsequies; interment. avval manzil pauṅhchānā, yā k . v. a. To perform the funeral rites; bury the dead. Kafan kāṭhī kar-ke avval manzil to pauṅhchāo .
adv. Firstly; imprimis ; in the first place; at first sight; in the first instance; prima facie. av'val , n. m. Beginning; commencement ( ādh ). Is kām kā avval bhī burā, ākhir bhī burā .
1. adj. First, best, principal; proper, fit. 2. s. m. Beginning. 3. awwal or اولا awwalā, adv. At first, in the first place.
subs: To remain or stand first in order; to maintain the first place
adv. From beginning to end, from first to last throughout
s.f. lit. 'First stage'; interment, obsequies; — awwal manzil pahuṅćānā, v.t. To perform (one's) obsequies, to bury.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوَّل (awwal), borrowed from Arabic أَوَّل (ʔawwal).

def: adv. At first, &c. = awwal, q.v. adj. First, prior, foremost, chief, greatest, highest, best, excellent; — s.m. The first or earlier part, beginning; — adv. At first, in the beginning, in the first place or instance
subs: To remain or stand first in order; to maintain the first place
adv. From beginning to end, from first to last throughout
s.f. lit. 'First stage'; interment, obsequies; — awwal manzil pahuṅćānā, v.t. To perform (one's) obsequies, to bury.

def: top priority; first priority
adj. First, former, ancient; — s.m. The ancients
more: adj. plur. (of اوّل) The first, the former; the ancients.
subs: s.m. The ancients and the moderns.

def: s.f. Priority, preëminence, superiority, excellence.
more: (from اول awwal ) s. f. Priority, preeminence, excellence, superiority.

def: adj. Unrepelled; unalarmed, not disconcerted.
part. adj. Unapparent, indistinct, invisible, unperceived, imperceptible, undetermined; — s.m. The Supreme Being or Universal Spirit; soul, nature, temperament.
adj. Not disturbed, composed, calm, firm.
adj. Not spread over or pervading the whole, not penetrating or permeating; special, peculiar.
s.f. Inadequate diffusion or extent (of a definition, &c.); — adj. Not diffusive, non-pervading.
adj. Undecided; without regulation, irregular.
part. adj. Not put in order, not regulated, irregular, undisciplined, unsteady.

adj. Not liable to change; unperishable, immutable, eternal; unwasted; economical, parsimonious; — s.m. An epithet of Vishṇu, or of Śiva; the Supreme Being or Universal Spirit; (in Gram.) an indeclinable word, particle.
n. lit . Imperishable. ( Sansk. Gram .) An indeclinable word; a particle.
adj. (in Gram.) Unchangeable, indeclinable; relating to an indeclinable word
part. (of ānā), Come
more: Conj. An interrogative particle; either; whether or not.
n. f. A female attendant on children; a lady's maid; nurse; maid-servant (in Anglo-Indian houses); āyah.
interj. used to interrogate or to express astonishment, ha! whether or not, whether, O!
subs: adj. (f. āʼī-gaʼī), Come and gone, gone, past, done with, spent, got rid of, disposed of
s.m. pl. Casual visitors, unexpected guests, guests
v.n. To be come and gone, to be gone, done with, be a thing of the past; to be disposed of, be settled; to be got rid of, spent, lost, done away with.

def: s.f. Female attendant on children or on a lady, nurse, lady's maid, ayah.
s.f. Nurse, &c. = āyā, q.v. s.m. Income, revenue, receipts, gain, profit.
more: s. m. A sentence of the ḳurʼān. See آيت āyat.

def: s.m. = āyat, q.v.

n. m. Plu. āë gaë. lit . he came, he went. One who has come and gone; a traveller who alights at a house; a chance or unexpected guest. Gehūṅ kahe, maiṅ baṛā sultān, Merā ho pūrī pakvān, Āë gaë kā rākhūṅ mān . Song. Ṭesū . The wheat says "I'm a mighty king, The dainty cakes from me do spring, I entertain the guests you bring." āyā gayā , adj. 1. Past; gone; spent; over. 2. Dissipated; got rid of; put an end to; done with. 3. Settled; adjusted; disposed of. Āī gaī bāt pār paṛī. āë gaē honā , v. n. 1. To lose; forfeit. Woh rupaë to āë gaë hue . 2. To be got rid of, settled. āge ānā , v. n. See under آگے ū'par ānā , v. n. See ā paṛnā (5). 1. To come up; ascend; mount. 2. To fall upon; come upon one's head. 3. Used as a double entendre. bīch meṅ ānā , v. n. 1. To lie, come, or get between; intervene. 2. To meddle; intermeddle; interfere. 3. To interpose; receive a blow aimed at another; take the place of; stand for. pās ānā , v. n. To come near ( ānā , 1, 2, 3). pīchhe ānā , v. n. To come after; follow; succeed. pesh ānā, āge ānā , v. n. 1. To come before; step forward; advance. 2. To treat one; behave; deal with. 3. To receive; welcome; greet. 4. To happen; occur; befal; betide. Pesh ānī hai wohī jo kuchh ki peshānī hai . What hath been writ before shall come before. tale, yā nīche ānā , v. n. 1. To come down; descend; dismount; etc. 2. To fall down; come under. 3. To be trodden, trampled, crushed under. 4. To take shelter, refuge. 5. To lie down ( double entendre ). 6. To come under one's power. 7. To suffer a loss. dekhne meṅ ānā , v. n. To appear; come in sight; become visible. tar'af ānā , v. n. To come or move towards; approach.
